channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: How can athletes play with ICDs, and what happens when it goes off?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how athletes with cardiac health issues are able to continue playing, the risks, and what happens when an ICD goes off.

item.title: Bellingham has fight to start for England - Tuchel
item.description: Jude Bellingham has a fight on his hands to feature in England's starting line-up at the World Cup, says manager Thomas Tuchel.

item.title: Eriksen 'in good spirits' after collapse
item.description: Christian Eriksen is "in good spirits" following his collapse against Ukraine and is expected to be discharged from hospital soon, says Denmark's national team doctor Morten Boesen.

item.title: Inside the fairytale journey of Scotland captain Robertson
item.description: Andy Robertson has come a long way since playing amateur football. Now he is edging towards become Scotland's most capped men's player.

item.title: Mourinho set for Real after Perez re-elected
item.description: Jose Mourinho is set to confirmed as Real Madrid's new head coach after Florentino Perez was re-elected as the club's president until 2030.

item.title: From dazzling winger to veteran who barely runs - the evolution of Messi
item.description: Lionel Messi is set for his sixth World Cup with Argentina. Guillem Balague looks at how the Argentine has evolved.

item.title: How Emslie went from giving birth to World Cup qualifiers inside six months
item.description: Scotland's Claire Emslie was back playing competitive football just five months after giving birth and is now targeting next year's World Cup finals in Brazil.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 1
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Ex-England midfielder Dyer named new Southend boss
item.description: Southend United appoint ex-Ipswich, Newcastle and England midfielder Kieron Dyer as their new head coach on an initial two-year contract.

item.title: Real Madrid to join Fernandes race - Monday's gossip
item.description: Real Madrid to join Mateus Fernandes race, Brighton reject Spurs' second Van Hecke bid and Harry Maguire could leave Manchester United.

item.title: Why Olise can be one of France's very best - Clichy
item.description: Former France international Gael Clichy explains why Bayern Munich's France forward Michael Olise could be the breakout star of this summer's World Cup.

item.title: How to follow the World Cup on the BBC
item.description: The biggest World Cup in history starts on 11 June and there are more ways to follow it on the BBC than ever before.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: World Cup 2026: All 48 squads confirmed
item.description: All 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: Emotion and celebration for Deschamps' home farewell
item.description: There will be "emotion and celebration" in Lille on Monday as manager Didier Deschamps prepares for his last match as France manager on home soil.

item.title: Shankland, Gunn and a midfield conundrum - Clarke's big decisions
item.description: Scotland head coach Steve Clarke has "some decisions to make" when it comes to his starting XI for their World Cup opener against Haiti on Sunday, but what are they?

item.title: Rice to be England's vice-captain at World Cup
item.description: Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice will be England's vice-captain for the World Cup.

item.title: Are Portugal better without Ronaldo - and is he undroppable?
item.description: As Cristiano Ronaldo prepares for his sixth World Cup with Portugal, BBC Sport take a closer look at his place in the team.

item.title: This was a training game - England and Tuchel now need to get serious
item.description: England have one more game to prepare for their World Cup campaign - and they must use it wisely, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Van Persie sacked by Feyenoord after 16 months
item.description: Feyenoord sack manager Robin van Persie after just one full season in charge of the Eredivisie club.

item.title: Scotland score four in first half to beat Bolivia
item.description: Watch the goals as Scotland score four in the first half to beat Bolivia in their final World Cup warm-up.

item.title: Highlights: Romania 2-1 Wales
item.description: Watch the best of the action as Romania defeat Wales 2-1 in Bucharest.

item.title: 'The things that forge them are similar' - the origins of Messi & Ronaldo
item.description: Both from humble beginnings, BBC Sport looks at how Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo became two of the greatest footballers of all time.

item.title: Watch: Weir hat-trick as Scotland thump Israel
item.description: Watch as Scotland thump Israel 6-0 in World Cup qualifying Group B4, with captain Caroline Weir scoring a hat-trick.

item.title: Highlights: Wales held to draw in Montenegro
item.description: Watch highlights as Montenegro and Wales draw in Podgorica in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Born in Leeds, crafted in Norway: How Haaland became nation's superstar
item.description: Erling Haaland has guided Norway to their first World Cup since 1998. So what is it like being a global name from a nation with a population of just 5.5m people?

item.title: Pre-World Cup friendlies - Messi rested & Leao sent off
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi is rested against Honduras, while Brazil, Germany, Belgium and Portugal each win warm-up games prior to the World Cup.

item.title: Iran's World Cup team lands in Mexico amid US visa row
item.description: All of Iran's group games are in the US, but players and support staff will have to fly in and out of the country on match day.

item.title: 'Draining' year out makes Roberts appreciate career
item.description: Burnley defender Connor Roberts returned in Wales' defeat by Romania with a fresh perspective after a "draining" year on the sidelines through injury.

item.title: Man Utd-bound Ederson replaces Wesley in Brazil squad
item.description: Brazil full-back Wesley is ruled out of the World Cup because of a thigh injury, with Manchester United-bound midfielder Ederson called up to replace him.

item.title: Watch: When Hagi's Romania thrashed Wales
item.description: Wales travel to Bucharest to face Romania on Saturday for the first time since their World Cup qualifying defeat in 1992.

item.title: Messi or Ronaldo - who is the greatest of all time?
item.description: Some of the greats of the game debate the long-asked question: Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo?

item.title: Bellamy urges Wales to dominate Romania
item.description: Head coach Craig Bellamy wants Wales team to try to dominate in Saturday's friendly against Romania in Bucharest.

item.title: Watch 24 Hours with Ella Toone on BBC iPlayer
item.description: Watch the trailer for a new BBC Sport documentary offering unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her father.

item.title: Shearer on Beckham, pressure and Argentina heartbreak
item.description: Alan Shearer reflects on his and England's performances at the 1998 World Cup.

item.title: Diana Ross & 'GOAT' Ronaldo - Richards watches World Cup moments
item.description: Former England defender and current pundit Micah Richards looks at some of the most memorable World Cup moments.

item.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo - watch the trailer
item.description: The story of how Lionel Messi v Cristiano Ronaldo became the rivalry that transformed football and defined a generation.

item.title: Liverpool will be enormous step for Iraola - Sutton
item.description: The Monday Night Club discuss Arne Slot's sacking from Liverpool and whether Andoni Iraola is the right replacement.

item.title: Belief but work to do - Wales' two years under Bellamy
item.description: BBC Sport Wales looks at Craig Bellamy's time in charge of Wales as he reaches the midway stage of his four-year deal with the Football Association of Wales.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Can you name every host?
item.description: Countries far and wide have hosted the festival of football since the first tournament in 1930. How many countries can you name?

item.title: Patient, precise, clinical - are Scotland ready to make World Cup mark?
item.description: Scotland thrash Bolivia and are in decent fettle one week out from the biggest game of their international lives.

item.title: O'Neill a 'no-brainer' for Celtic – now major rebuild awaits
item.description: With Martin O'Neill poised to be confirmed as Celtic manager after two interim spells last season, how big is the summer rebuild he is tasked with?

item.title: Brighton agree £21.5m deal for winger Yohanna
item.description: Brighton agree to sign Nigerian winger Zadok Yohanna from AIK Stockholm in a deal worth £21.5m.

item.title: Mr Irreplaceable and Ballon d'Or contender - is this Kane's time?
item.description: Harry Kane has been described as irreplaceable for England, but will the World Cup provide the stage for him to prove he is now the best?

item.title: France's Camavinga attends Harvard after World Cup omission
item.description: France midfielder Eduardo Camavinga takes a Harvard Business School course after missing out on Didier Deschamps' World Cup squad.

item.title: The pioneer in an unlikely World Cup team
item.description: When Desmond Armstrong faced the media at the World Cup in 1990, the first question he got was one that would stick in his mind for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Lightning halts pre-World Cup friendly in Texas for two hours
item.description: Saudi Arabia's World Cup warm-up game against Puerto Rico in Texas is halted for nearly two hours because of extreme weather.

item.title: Sullivan steps down at West Ham in wake of historical allegations
item.description: West Ham United co-owner David Sullivan steps down as joint chairman of the club with immediate effect following a joint investigation by BBC Panorama and the Times newspaper into his behaviour.

item.title: Which World Cup team are you? Take our quiz
item.description: Whether you're a neutral in need of a team to support or just fancy following a second side, BBC Sport has got you covered.

item.title: Arsenal approach PSG defender Mbemba
item.description: Arsenal make an approach to sign teenage Paris St-Germain defender Emmanuel Mbemba on a free transfer this summer.

item.title: Gemmill beats McTominay in vote for your most iconic Scotland goal
item.description: The votes are in and the contest is now over - you have decided that Archie Gemmill's solo effort against the Netherlands at the 1978 World Cup is Scotland's Most Iconic Goal.

item.title: The anatomy of a Golden Boot winner - and who could win it in 2026?
item.description: A look at the common attributes of World Cup Golden Boot winners and the claims of the leading 2026 candidates examined.

item.title: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait
item.description: Iraq return to the World Cup with ambitions of delivering stronger performances and results than first time round in 1986.

item.title: Potter's Zlatan texts, overcoming failure & finding joy in Sweden
item.description: Graham Potter talks about his joy and pride in taking Sweden to the World Cup after hurt of Chelsea and West Ham dismissals.

item.title: Superstar in Spain, doubted in Brazil: Will Vinicius convince a nation?
item.description: Vinicius Junior is considered one of the best players in the world, but can he make the Brazilian public fall in love with him at the World Cup?

item.title: From EFL to World Cup - 11 players to look out for
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at 11 EFL players to watch out for at this summer's World Cup.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: 'Devlin torn between Hearts and big-money offers' - gossip
item.description: Cammy Devlin is reportedly emotionally torn between a new Heart of Midlothian contract and rival offers, while Robbie Keane is said to have been told he could yet be Celtic manager some day.

item.title: Spain expose England's flaws in painful defeat
item.description: England just needed to avoid defeat against Spain - instead they suffered their heaviest loss in 17 years, but it is not time to panic yet.

item.title: Wales aim to right their wrongs against Czechs
item.description: Wales' trip to Montenegro did not go according to plan and, as a result, the path to the Women's World Cup is unchanged.

item.title: How England can still qualify for the Women's World Cup
item.description: England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland are all vying for places at the Women's World Cup - but how can they get there with one round of group games left in European qualifying?

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.
